title: ISE Group-Based Policy (GBP / Microsegmentation) Effective-Policy Drift

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX ITGC change management — segmentation policy changes.
CM-3 configuration change control — segmentation matrix.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Modify a single SGT-matrix cell. UC-17.1.76 detects drift on next run.

dataSources: ISE configuration data via OpenAPI (`/ers/config/sgmatrix`, `/ers/config/sgt`) — periodic export of the SGT-to-SGT (source-to-destination) policy matrix into a Splunk lookup `ise_sgt_matrix.csv`. ISE syslog *Authorization* — codes `5402`, `5403` for dynamic SGT-policy compilation events. Compare current matrix against baseline `ise_sgt_matrix_baseline.csv`.
spl:
| inputlookup ise_sgt_matrix.csv 
| join type=outer source_sgt destination_sgt [| inputlookup ise_sgt_matrix_baseline.csv | rename action as baseline_action]
| eval drift=if(action!=baseline_action, "DRIFTED", "ok")
| where drift=="DRIFTED"
description: Detects unauthorised changes to the SGT-to-SGT effective policy matrix. Compares each cell of the current matrix against the approved baseline and flags any drift.
value: The GBP matrix is the heart of microsegmentation policy. A single cell change (e.g., `Employees → DC_PCI` from deny to allow) can compromise an entire segmentation strategy. Drift detection catches unauthorized changes within the polling interval.
implementation: Daily. Requires a curated baseline CSV checked into version control.
visualization: Drift cells over time; current matrix vs. baseline visualization.
knownFalsePositives: (a) Approved baseline change pending update — coordinate with policy engineering.
mitreAttack: N/A (policy hygiene — measures Group-Based Policy hit counts, no adversary technique)
grandmaExplanation: The microsegmentation matrix is a big spreadsheet of which device groups can talk to which. We check it daily against the approved version and flag any cell that has changed.
